title: Intersection of Linked Lists
hints: If the two linked lists intersect, they will share the same tail nodes.
The key insight is that if you traverse both lists, they will meet at the intersection point.
Use two pointers, one starting from each list. When one pointer reaches the end, switch it to the other list.
If the lists intersect, the pointers will meet at the intersection node.
If the lists don't intersect, both pointers will become null at the same time.
This approach works because it equalizes the path lengths by switching lists.
